---
title: "2.11 Nested Iteration"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Nested Loops

### A loop can run inside another loop

![Nested loops overview](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-11-nested-iteration/nestedloops.png){fig-align="center" width="44%"}

In nested iteration:

::: {.tight-list}
- the outer loop controls larger repetitions
- the inner loop restarts for each outer-loop pass
- the inner loop must finish before the outer loop advances
:::

## Rows and Columns

### Nested loops naturally model two dimensions

```java
for (int row = 1; row <= 3; row++)
{
    for (int col = 1; col <= 5; col++)
    {
        System.out.print("*");
    }
    System.out.println();
}
```

This is why nested loops are the standard pattern for:

::: {.tight-list}
- grids
- rectangles of output
- row/column reasoning
- repeated shapes
:::

## Tracing Nested Iteration

### Outer iterations and inner iterations are separate counts

Students need to answer two questions:

::: {.tight-list}
- how many times does the outer loop run?
- how many times does the inner loop run for each outer pass?
:::

That structure is the foundation for later loop-analysis work.

## Nested Loops with Turtles

### Repeated shapes plus a small turn create larger patterns

```java
for (int i = 1; i <= 12; i++)
{
    for (int sides = 1; sides <= 4; sides++)
    {
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turn(90);
    }
    yertle.turn(30);
}
```

The inner loop draws one shape.  
The outer loop repeats the shape with a new orientation.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- tracing rows and columns in printed-star patterns
- distinguishing outer-loop count from inner-loop count
- adjusting nested loop bounds for different rectangles
- turtle-based repeated-shape reasoning
- <span class="term">2.11.2 Coding Challenge: Turtle Snowflakes</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define nested iteration as a loop inside another loop
- explain that the inner loop restarts for each outer iteration
- connect nested loops to row/column style output
- trace how many times inner work is repeated
- explain the role split between outer pattern control and inner shape control
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
